About the Program

Bridging Sri Lankan enterprise
to U.S. standards

Sri Lankan SMEs have the ambition, the products, and the talent to engage with U.S. partners. What they often lack is institutional readiness — the documentation discipline, contractual literacy, and transparency standards that U.S. partners expect as a baseline.

The U.S. Enterprise Bridge addresses that gap directly. Through public masterclasses, an intensive 20-SME cohort, and structured dialogue with U.S.-based experts, we translate American small business system principles into practical, locally relevant programming.

Funded by

Who We Are

Built from the ground up
by Sri Lankan entrepreneurs

UnicornFactory started in 2021 with a simple YouTube channel — a free, accessible resource teaching Sri Lankan youth the fundamentals of entrepreneurship. From ideation and idea validation to building and launching a startup, the content was designed for anyone who had a business idea but didn't know where to start.

The series grew into a television talk show on Dialog Channel One, featuring Sri Lanka's most prominent entrepreneurs, a podcast, and eventually a full 16-week Startup Bootcamp — a hands-on accelerator selecting high-potential founders annually, completely free of charge.

Funded by American Spaces Sri Lanka through the U.S. Embassy, UnicornFactory became the country's most accessible entrepreneurship platform — empowering aspiring founders from Colombo to Jaffna.

The U.S. Enterprise Bridge is the next chapter — taking that same mission to Sri Lanka's established SME community, preparing them for meaningful engagement with U.S. partners and the global marketplace.

How It Works

Four phases.
One transformation.

A structured 5-month journey combining public education with intensive cohort training — designed to build lasting SME readiness for U.S. engagement.

01
Month 1
Awareness & Recruitment
Public masterclasses in 5 cities. Open-enrollment workshops covering U.S. business culture, documentation standards, and export readiness fundamentals.
02
Month 2
Cohort Selection & Bootcamp
20 SMEs selected through a competitive application. Intensive 4-day bootcamp covering U.S. regulatory frameworks, compliance, and business communication.
03
Months 3–4
Deep-Dive Masterclasses
Expert-led sessions on supply chain standards, intellectual property, contract negotiation, and financial transparency expected by U.S. partners.
04
Month 5
U.S.–LK Dialogue & Showcase
Structured dialogue with U.S.-based SMEs and trade experts. Cohort showcase where businesses present their readiness and explore partnership opportunities.
Inaugural Event

Launch Reception
May 2026

Join us for the official launch of the U.S. Enterprise Bridge — an evening reception bringing together Sri Lanka's SME community, U.S. Embassy representatives, and business leaders to mark the beginning of this national initiative.

The evening will feature program announcements, keynote addresses, and networking. The SME Cohort application window opens on the night. All are welcome — this is a public event.

Register for the Reception
SME Cohort

20 businesses.
One transformation.

The core of the U.S. Enterprise Bridge is an intensive cohort of 20 Sri Lankan SMEs selected through a competitive application. Cohort members receive structured training, expert mentorship, and direct access to U.S. business networks.

Workshops are led by practitioners — Sri Lankan entrepreneurs who have navigated U.S. partnerships, and U.S. advisors who understand exactly what American companies need from foreign partners.

U.S. Business Culture & Communication
How American SMEs think, communicate, and build trust — the unwritten rules of U.S. business relationships
Documentation & Compliance Readiness
Financial statements, legal structures, certifications, and compliance frameworks expected by U.S. partners
Export & Trade Standards
Product standards, labelling requirements, IP protection, and supply chain visibility for U.S. market access
Contract Literacy & Negotiation
Understanding U.S.-style commercial contracts, payment terms, liability clauses, and negotiation norms
Who Should Apply

Is your business
enterprise-ready?

This cohort is for established Sri Lankan SMEs — businesses already operating and looking to take the next step toward U.S. market engagement.

  • Registered Sri Lankan business operating for 2+ years
  • Annual turnover of LKR 5M or more
  • Active interest in U.S. partnership, export, or investment
  • Owner or C-suite representative committed to all sessions
  • Product or service viable for U.S. market
  • Openness to transparency, documentation, and compliance improvement

"This is for businesses that have proven themselves locally and are asking — what do we need to do to be taken seriously by an American partner?"

— Jehan Wijesinghe, Founder, UnicornFactory

Apply for the Cohort
